extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won the Stage 4 ITT, taking yellow after intermediate checks suggested YATES could win, with Jumbo-Visma sweeping the podium 1-2-3.
tactical_patterns: YATES fastest at intermediate checkpoint on flat terrain but faded on difficult climb finish, revealing TT pacing weakness.
Jumbo-Visma swept podium (VAN AERT, ROGLIC, DENNIS) revealing team depth forced rivals to race three separate opponents simultaneously.
VAN AERT beat ROGLIC despite ROGLIC being designated GC leader, showing team hierarchy remains fluid in stage races.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: intermediate split faster than DENNIS on flat suggests improved TT form, but climbing finish exposed limitations
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: beaten by teammate VAN AERT despite being designated GC leader, 4 seconds faster than DENNIS
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: positive: Australian TT champion held hot seat long into stage, third-best shows continued world-class ITT form
rider_signals.KUNG Stefan: negative: European TT champion finished 4 seconds down on DENNIS, underwhelming for reigning continental champion
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: negative: lost yellow by 30 seconds despite only 1-second deficit, revealing climbing TT weakness for rouleur
